description:
To properly set the group owner of /var/log/lastlog, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /var/log/lastlog
rationale:
Unauthorized disclosure of the contents of the /var/log/lastlog file can reveal system data to
attackers, thus compromising its confidentiality.
ocil_clause:
/var/log/lastlog does not have a group owner of
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /var/log/lastlog, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/lastlog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: root
fixtext:
Change the group of the directory /var/log/lastlog to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chgrp root /var/log/lastlog
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /var/log/lastlog directory must be group-owned by root.
title: Verify Group Who Owns lastlog Command
